sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    color = n['color']
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    if color in ['b', 'c', 'M']:
        print(f"{color}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check ascii around overlap area
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n--- Rows 0-8, cols 18-42 ---")
for i in range(0, 9):
    if i < len(ascii_lines):
        print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][18:42]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me re-examine. The blue was at rows 16-19. I moved UP 7 times (28 rows / 4 = 7 moves), getting to rows 8-11... but the output shows rows 12-15. Let me re-check.
Actually, 16 - 28 = -12, which would be negative. So the blue can't move that far up. Let me recalculate:

- After 1 UP: rows 12-15 (moved 4 rows)
- After 2 UPs: rows 8-11
- After 3 UPs: rows 4-7
